physical._comment_m2A: Gluon mass parameter, tuned to the scaling regime (angle-resolved S0/S1/SPhi vertices + angle-resolved ZA4tadpole). The scaling separatrix sits at m2A_init ~= -91.9: as m2A approaches it from above the IR gluon mass tunes through 0 and the ghost dressing is driven toward the scaling solution -- Zc(0) drops from ~0.91 (massive branch) to ~0.53 at m2A=-91.9 (below the SP value 0.577), then crosses to the Higgs branch (diverges) for m2A<=-91.92. m2A=-91.85 lands just on the scaling side (IR m2A ~ +0.01, Zc(0) ~ 0.75, robust); push toward -91.9 for the deepest ghost enhancement. The flow is numerically stable on the massive side (the collinear singularity is confined to one grid corner by the S0/S1/SPhi gridding).
